description:
Pre-0.3.8 review paths didn't emit the `no-code` marker on gate/empty-squash reviews, so downstream repos (e.g. lernie, ~/dev/lernie) have state-branch history like:

  state: review bl-xxxx
  state: close bl-xxxx - Gate: ...

which `detect_half_push` (src/commands/sync.rs) flags forever — there's no retroactive way to mark those reviews as no-code. `bl repair --fix` today only cleans orphan claims/worktrees, not stale half-push warnings.

=== What to build ===

New state-branch subject: `state: forget-half-push bl-xxxx`. An empty commit on balls/tasks that acknowledges a close whose delivery never landed on main. `detect_half_push` subtracts any id with a matching forget commit before emitting warnings.

New repair surface: `bl repair --forget-half-push <ID>` (repeatable) and `bl repair --forget-half-push-all`. The first retracts a single id; the second retracts every id currently flagged. Both write empty state-branch commits and then return clean.

Rationale for the state-branch-commit design (vs a config file list):
- Matches §7.4's "durable, git-visible record of the recovery. No silent mutation of history."
- Syncs across machines via the state branch without a new transport.
- Auditable: `git log balls/tasks` shows when and why the warning was retracted.
- Symmetric with `no-code` — both are metadata-only markers on the state branch.

=== Testing ===

Add tests under tests/sync_half_push.rs (or a new tests/repair_forget.rs):
- forget-half-push for a known half-pushed id removes the warning
- forget-half-push --all retracts every current warning
- forget-half-push for a non-flagged id is a no-op (doesn't create a commit, or creates one that's harmless — pick one)
- warning stays away after a subsequent `bl sync`
- the unit test in sync.rs covers the subject-parsing helper

cargo test + existing CI checks green.

=== Docs ===

- docs/SPEC-orphan-branch-state.md §7.4: add the forget-half-push action as a third recovery option, alongside retry and rollback.
- SKILL.md: one-line mention under the repair command entry.

=== Out of scope ===

- Rewriting or amending old `state: review bl-xxxx` commits to add `no-code` retroactively (history rewrite on a shared branch is a non-starter per SPEC §7.4).
- Any change to how new reviews are marked — 0.3.8 already emits `no-code` correctly.
- A corresponding "forget" command for other warning classes — this is half-push-specific.
title: repair: retract stale half-push warnings
